/**
 * CustomCursor — the app-shell cursor chrome (S05).
 *
 * Thin chrome alias over the S04 primitive MagneticCursor so there is exactly
 * ONE implementation of the cursor mechanics: a tight accent dot + lagging
 * ring lerped by a single gsap.ticker callback into prebuilt quickSetters —
 * no standalone rAF, zero per-frame allocations, no per-frame
 * getBoundingClientRect (scar #10 doctrine lives in MagneticCursor.jsx).
 *
 * Mount ONCE in RootLayout — it is a singleton overlay that persists across
 * routes (shell-level, never inside a per-route context — scar #9 awareness).
 *
 * Gating (scar #8 + §3.4): renders null on coarse pointer OR
 * prefers-reduced-motion OR mobile (<768). Native cursor untouched on those
 * tiers; every target stays a plain tappable element.
 *
 * Magnetic-target contract: the ring grows over `a, button, [data-cursor]` —
 * opt extra targets in via data-cursor="magnet". MagneticButton forwards the
 * attribute through ...rest; the cursor only READS it on pointerover while the
 * button owns its own transform, so the two cooperate without double-binding.
 */
import MagneticCursor from "../motion/MagneticCursor.jsx";

export default function CustomCursor(props) {
  return <MagneticCursor {...props} />;
}

input.new_string:
---

## Global chrome contract (S05)
<!-- appended by sprint 05 -->

The persistent app shell. `src/pages/RootLayout.jsx` renders, in order:
`<Preloader/>` + `<CustomCursor/>` (gated) + `<Navbar/>` + `<main id="main">`
(relative — the S13 wipe-overlay host, scar #4) wrapping the transition seam +
`<Footer/>`. Routed page content mounts in the seam BETWEEN Navbar and Footer.
S02's `StubNav.jsx` / `StubFooter.jsx` are DELETED — superseded by the chrome
below.

| piece | path | contract |
|---|---|---|
| Navbar | `src/components/chrome/Navbar.jsx` | `<NavLink>` rows from `NAV` + MagneticButton Calendly CTA from `LINKS.calendly`. Condense-on-scroll = ONE shell-level ScrollTrigger (`id: "navbar-condense"`, start 64 → "max") flipping `data-condensed` on the `<header>` — lives in the component's OWN `gsap.context`, **NEVER in `useRouteScrollContext`** (a per-route context would revert it on first nav and freeze the navbar). Mobile toggle = `[data-nav-toggle]`, menu panel = `#mobile-menu` (conditional render, instant). |
| Footer | `src/components/chrome/Footer.jsx` | Global on every route. Hosts `#newsletter` (inside `<footer>`), the Sender mount `[data-sender-form-id="axkAXn"]` (+ `sender-form-field` class) with the LOCKED polling-render lifecycle (`window.senderForms.render/destroy`, 100ms × 50 poll, StrictMode-safe: cancel + destroy + `replaceChildren()` on cleanup), the ONE company-LinkedIn link, `mailto:hello@kartar.ai`, a Calendly CTA, NAV links — all from `src/data/*`. |
| CustomCursor | `src/components/chrome/CustomCursor.jsx` | Thin alias over `src/components/motion/MagneticCursor.jsx` — ONE cursor implementation, mounted ONCE in RootLayout (singleton overlay; the S04 `/lab` local mount was removed). Gated: renders `null` on coarse pointer / mobile (<768) / reduced-motion. Ring node carries `data-magnetic-cursor`. |
| Wordmark | `src/components/chrome/Wordmark.jsx` | `BRAND.wordmark` → "kartar" + accent "AI". No saffron (anti-wander rule 1). |
| CTA→newsletter helper | `src/lib/goToNewsletter.js` → `goToNewsletter()` | THE shared helper (locked behavior). Footer is global so it works from ANY route: `window.__lenis.scrollTo("#newsletter", {offset:-24})`, `scrollIntoView` fallback (instant under reduced-motion). Returns `false` if the anchor is missing. Dev/QA global: `window.__goToNewsletter` (DEV-only, DCE'd from prod). **S06's CtaBanner imports this — never hand-roll the scroll.** Home's stub `#cta` button is already wired through it. |
| Magnetic-target opt-in | `[data-cursor="magnet"]` | The cursor ring grows over `a, button, [data-cursor]`; opt extra/explicit targets in via `data-cursor="magnet"`. MagneticButton forwards it via `...rest`; cursor only READS on pointerover — no double-binding. |

**New QA-keyed selectors:** `[data-route-seam]` (the seam wrapper — verify
asserts `transform: none` + `overflowY: visible`, scar #2), `data-condensed`
(navbar scroll state), `[data-nav-toggle]`, `#mobile-menu`,
`[data-cursor="magnet"]`.

**Perf note:** `framer-motion` (existing dep) now ships in the initial bundle
for the seam — initial JS 167.07 KB gz (budget ≤ 220 KB). Still zero `three`
bytes on every route.

---

## Transition timing
<!-- appended by sprint 13 -->

S13 fills the page-transition timing so QA waits long enough and confirms no
trigger leak across nav (scar #9). Reduced-motion = instant cut.

- Transition system home: _(S13)_
- Total in+out duration (ms) QA must wait: _(S13)_
- Refresh/kill ordering across the seam: _(S13)_

<!-- appended by sprint 05 (the structural seam S13 fills) -->
**S05 seam (live, no-op):** `src/pages/RootLayout.jsx` — `<AnimatePresence
mode="wait" initial={false}>` keyed on `location.pathname` around a
`motion.div[data-route-seam]` containing a **FrozenOutlet** (captures
`useOutlet()` once per mount so an exiting child keeps rendering the OLD page —
prevents double-mount through the swap and preserves scar-#9 ordering: old
route's context reverts before the new route's effects).
- The seam wrapper must NEVER gain transform/overflow/height that captures
  scroll (scar #2) — verify.mjs asserts this per route.
- The seam does NOT kill triggers — that stays in `useRouteScrollContext`'s
  revert. S13 adds the wipe as an `absolute inset-0` overlay on the relative
  `<main id="main">` and calls `lenis.scrollTo(0,{immediate:true})` +
  `refreshSoon()` at cover-peak — the commented **"S13 TRANSITION HOOK POINT"**
  marker in RootLayout.jsx is the exact spot.
- Gate transitions on `window.__preloaderDone` / the `preloaderDone` event,
  never on the preloader node existing.

# CONTRACTS — the living technical contract

The binding interface between sprints. It grows: most sections below are
skeletons now; the named sprint **appends** to them at runtime. When you add a
selector, id, data-attr, global, or API, you MUST record it here in the same
commit, so QA assertions and later sprints stay in sync.

**Authority:** `PROTOCOL > CONTRACTS > DIRECTION/TOKENS > sprint file`. This doc
beats DIRECTION and the sprint file on any **locked id / selector / integration
id**. It does not override PROTOCOL.

**How to append:** find your sprint's section, replace its `(empty — …)`
placeholder or add rows beneath the existing ones. Keep the
`<!-- appended by sprint NN -->` marker so the next author knows where their
turn begins. Never delete a prior sprint's entries; if you supersede one, strike
it and add the replacement with a note.

---

## Route map
<!-- appended by sprint 02 (then refined by later page sprints 06-09) -->

The router IA. S02 fills the real rows from `design/DIRECTION.md`'s IA table.
Page sprints (S06–S09) fill in their owned section ids. QA's
`scripts/qa/routes.mjs` mirrors this table — keep them in sync.

| path | component (src path) | owned section ids / anchors | showpiece mount(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/` | `src/pages/Home.jsx` | `#hero #pipeline #industries #services #testimonials #cta` | `data-showpiece="hero"` (CSS/SVG, S10) + `data-showpiece="pipeline"` (WebGL, S11) |
| `/work` | `src/pages/Work.jsx` | `#case-studies #industries #recap` | `data-showpiece="skyline"` (WebGL, S11/S12) |
| `/studio` | `src/pages/Studio.jsx` | `#process #team #manifesto` | — (zero-GL route) |
| `/contact` | `src/pages/Contact.jsx` | `#contact` | — (zero-GL route) |

<!-- appended by sprint 02 -->
**S02 router map notes (rewrote the skeleton per DIRECTION §IA):**
- Router: `src/App.jsx` = `<Routes>` with a layout route (`RootLayout.jsx` →

## Scroll/motion backbone API (S03)
<!-- appended by sprint 03 -->

The backbone every later sprint composes on. Files:
`src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx` · `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js` ·
`src/lib/motion.js` · `src/components/Preloader.jsx`.

### `useRouteScrollContext()` — `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js`
```js
const { scopeRef, add, refreshSoon } = useRouteScrollContext();
```
- **EVERY page component (S06–S13) MUST call this** and register ALL its
  scroll work through it — no ad-hoc ScrollTriggers outside a route context.
- `scopeRef` → attach to the page's root element (plain block, never
  transformed — scar #2). All 5 current pages already do this.
- `add(fn)` → run `fn` inside the route's `gsap.context`. Safe to call from
  section effects that run BEFORE the page's context exists (child effects run
  first): callbacks queue and flush into the context at creation. Sections that
  unmount independently of the route must manage their own nested
  `gsap.context` + revert.
- On route unmount the context `revert()`s — every trigger/tween dies BEFORE
  the next route's effects run (scar #9, proven by the verify.mjs 3×-cycle
  assertion).
- The hook auto-creates one zero-cost **route-sentinel** ScrollTrigger
  (id `route-sentinel`) per route — that is why `__stCount()` baseline is 1,
  not 0. Do not remove it; the leak assertion measures against it.
- **Scar #1 reminder** (in the hook's JSDoc): never `self.kill()` inside
  `onUpdate`/`onToggle` — `gsap.delayedCall(0, () => self.kill())`.

### `refreshSoon(delaySec = 0.15)` — same module, also exported standalone
- THE one coordinated, debounced `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` channel (scar #3).
  Calls coalesce. Already wired: `document.fonts.ready` + `preloaderDone` →
  refresh; every route mount → refresh; nav scroll-reset → refresh.
- **S13 calls `refreshSoon()` after its transition-in completes.** Sections
  NEVER call `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` directly.

### SmoothScroll provider — `src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx`
- Mounted ONCE in `src/main.jsx` (inside `BrowserRouter`, outside the Outlet).
  Lenis scrolls the REAL window, driven from `gsap.ticker`
  (`lagSmoothing(0)`); survives navigation; on route change does
  `lenis.scrollTo(0, { immediate: true })` + `refreshSoon()`.
- `useLenis()` context hook → the live instance (null on native tiers/early).
- Reduced-motion OR mobile (≤768) ⇒ **no Lenis at all** (native scroll tier).

### Preloader — `src/components/Preloader.jsx` (mounted in `RootLayout`)
- `[data-preloader]` veil, z-[100]; "All Is. Now" assemble + 0→100 counter on
  the drafting grid; pure CSS/GSAP, no deps. Total ≈2.4s.
- Once per session: sessionStorage key **`k-preloaded`** (set on completion
  AND on every skip path).
- Skip paths (render null, no veil): session flag · `?skippreload` ·
  `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- **`preloaderDone`** — `window` CustomEvent dispatched on EVERY path
  (completed or skipped) + `window.__preloaderDone = true`. The refresh
  coordinator and `?jump` wait on it. S13's transition system must NOT gate on
  the preloader existing — gate on the event/flag.
- Scroll-lock while shown: `documentElement.style.overflow = "hidden"` +
  `lenis.stop()`; both restored on exit.

### Motion constants — `src/lib/motion.js` (TOKENS §5, live)
- `EASE.PRIMARY` = CustomEase **"kPrimary"** (`cubic-bezier(0.7,0,0.2,1)`,
  registered at module load) · `EASE.SETTLE` = `"expo.out"` · `EASE.SNAP` =
  `"back.out(1.4)"` (never on opacity). CSS twin: `EASE_CSS_PRIMARY`.
- `DUR.XS/SM/MD/LG/SCENE` = 0.3/0.5/0.6/0.7/1.0s · `STAGGER.LINE/CELL` =
  0.06/0.04. One easing language — import from here only (motion law 1).
- Legacy exports (`EASE_OUT`, `revealVariants`, …) remain for pre-rebuild
  components only; they die with the old components (S05+).

# CONTRACTS — the living technical contract

The binding interface between sprints. It grows: most sections below are
skeletons now; the named sprint **appends** to them at runtime. When you add a
selector, id, data-attr, global, or API, you MUST record it here in the same
commit, so QA assertions and later sprints stay in sync.

**Authority:** `PROTOCOL > CONTRACTS > DIRECTION/TOKENS > sprint file`. This doc
beats DIRECTION and the sprint file on any **locked id / selector / integration
id**. It does not override PROTOCOL.

**How to append:** find your sprint's section, replace its `(empty — …)`
placeholder or add rows beneath the existing ones. Keep the
`<!-- appended by sprint NN -->` marker so the next author knows where their
turn begins. Never delete a prior sprint's entries; if you supersede one, strike
it and add the replacement with a note.

---

## Route map
<!-- appended by sprint 02 (then refined by later page sprints 06-09) -->

The router IA. S02 fills the real rows from `design/DIRECTION.md`'s IA table.
Page sprints (S06–S09) fill in their owned section ids. QA's
`scripts/qa/routes.mjs` mirrors this table — keep them in sync.

| path | component (src path) | owned section ids / anchors | showpiece mount(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/` | `src/pages/Home.jsx` | `#hero #pipeline #industries #services #testimonials #cta` | `data-showpiece="hero"` (CSS/SVG, S10) + `data-showpiece="pipeline"` (WebGL, S11) |
| `/work` | `src/pages/Work.jsx` | `#case-studies #industries #recap` | `data-showpiece="skyline"` (WebGL, S11/S12) |
| `/studio` | `src/pages/Studio.jsx` | `#process #team #manifesto` | — (zero-GL route) |
| `/contact` | `src/pages/Contact.jsx` | `#contact` | — (zero-GL route) |

<!-- appended by sprint 02 -->
**S02 router map notes (rewrote the skeleton per DIRECTION §IA):**
- Router: `src/App.jsx` = `<Routes>` with a layout route (`RootLayout.jsx` →
  `StubNav` + `<Outlet/>` + `StubFooter`); catch-all `*` → `NotFound.jsx`
  (unique id `#not-found`). `BrowserRouter basename="/"` is in `src/main.jsx`.
- DIRECTION §IA reconciliation: the **CustomsIQ pipeline** showpiece lives on
  **Home (`#pipeline`)**, the **9-industry skyline** on **Work (`#industries`)**
  — matching S01's supersession note. Selector NAMES (`hero`/`pipeline`/

- `#newsletter` lives in `StubFooter` (global) and resolves from every route;
  the Home `#cta` button + CTA banner target it via `href="#newsletter"`.
- Stubs are data-complete but **motion-free** (no Lenis/ScrollTrigger yet — S03).
  `RootLayout`'s `<Outlet/>` wrapper is a PLAIN block, never a transformed
  scroll container (scar #2). The page-transition seam is S05/S13.
- Chrome (`StubNav`/`StubFooter`) is intentionally MINIMAL — S05 replaces both
  with the real Navbar/Footer; the `#newsletter` anchor + Sender mount
  (`data-sender-form-id="axkAXn"`) must survive that swap.

> If DIRECTION chooses a different route count/names, S02 rewrites this whole
> table and the orchestrator re-points S06–S09 from it. `#newsletter` must live
> on exactly one route and be reachable cross-route from the CTA (see
> Integration contracts).

## Motion primitives API
<!-- appended by sprint 04 -->

`src/components/motion/*` — the reusable primitives every page sprint composes.
S04 fills the table: one row per primitive with `import path`, `props`, the gate
it applies (reduced-motion/mobile → static final state), and a one-line
behavior. Page sprints compose ONLY these — no ad-hoc GSAP in sections except
via the route-scroll-context.

| primitive | import path | props | gate | behavior |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `SplitLines` (aliases `MaskText`, `SplitTextReveal`) | `src/components/motion/SplitLines.jsx` | `as="h2", children:string, mode="lines"\|"words", delay, stagger, className` | reduced/mobile → plain string, fully visible | waits `document.fonts.ready` (scar #3), splits words → groups lines by offsetTop → masked yPercent reveal, once, holds. `data-split="done"` on complete |
| `InkReveal` | `src/components/motion/InkReveal.jsx` | `as, from="up"\|"left"\|"right"\|"none", delay, duration, start, className, children` | reduced/mobile → final state | ghost (opacity 0 + TRAVEL px + clip inset) → inked strike, EASE.PRIMARY, once, holds forever. Root `relative` (scar #4), `data-revealed="true"` on complete |
| `DraftLine` | `src/components/motion/DraftLine.jsx` | `orient="h"\|"v"\|"diag", accent, thickness, scrub, className` | reduced/mobile → fully drawn | SVG stroke-dashoffset draw, once or scrubbed-with-latch (scar #5); strokes `currentColor` from token text classes |
| `DimensionMark` | `src/components/motion/DimensionMark.jsx` | `label, side="left"\|"top", accent, className` | reduced/mobile → drawn + visible | mono micro-label + ~48px leader line draws then label inks in, once |
| `SnapStack` (alias `CardStack`) | `src/components/motion/SnapStack.jsx` | `stagger, start, className, children` (direct children = cards; caller owns grid layout) | reduced/mobile → plain stacked cards | cards arrive from right + alternating rotation, EASE.SNAP on transforms ONLY, EASE.PRIMARY on opacity, once, holds |
| `AxisRail` (alias `HorizontalPin`) | `src/components/motion/AxisRail.jsx` | `markers, className, children` (each child = one panel) | reduced/mobile → vertical stack, NO pin | pins root `+=(n-1)*100%`, scrub 0.6, track xPercent lateral travel + accent progress rail + mono counter; scar-#5 latch sets `data-latched="end"\|"start"` |
| `KineticMarquee` | `src/components/motion/KineticMarquee.jsx` | `items:string[], speed=40, velocityFactor, skewOnVelocity=false (ONLY the industries band may set true), className, renderItem` | reduced/mobile → static flex-wrap list | duplicated row driven by ONE gsap.ticker reading `scrollBus.velocity` (the one shared velocity — see `src/lib/scrollBus.js`); pauses offscreen via onToggle flag |
| `MagneticButton` | `src/components/motion/MagneticButton.jsx` | `as="button"\|"a", strength=0.35, className, children, ...rest` | coarse pointer OR reduced/mobile → plain tap target | quickTo magnet toward cursor (root ×strength, label ×strength/2), rect cached per pointerenter, settles on leave |
| `MagneticCursor` | `src/components/motion/MagneticCursor.jsx` | `size=32` | coarse/reduced/mobile → renders `null` (native cursor untouched) | fixed dot+ring lerped via one gsap.ticker + quickSetters; ring scales over `a, button, [data-cursor]`. Ring carries `data-magnetic-cursor` |
| `CountStrike` (supersedes legacy `CountUp.jsx`) | `src/components/motion/CountStrike.jsx` | `to, from=0, decimals, prefix, suffix, duration=DUR.SCENE, format?, className, as="span"` | reduced/mobile → final number immediately | DOM ships the FINAL formatted value (en-IN grouping, `tabular-nums`); motion tier rewinds + counts up once at center, writes textContent (no setState), `data-struck="true"` |
| `ScrubScene` | `src/components/motion/ScrubScene.jsx` | `length="+=200%", pin=true, scrub=0.6, onProgress(p), latch=true, cssVar=false, className, children` | reduced/mobile → NO pin, children plain, `onProgress(1)` once, `data-state="final"` | **THE generic showpiece harness (S10/S11 build on it).** Pins root, scrubs, calls `onProgress(self.progress)` imperatively each update — consumers must NEVER setState per frame and must defer any kill via `gsap.delayedCall` (scar #1). Scar-#5 latch commits 1/0 on leave/leaveBack |
| `ParallaxLayer` | `src/components/motion/ParallaxLayer.jsx` | `speed=-0.15 (negative = counter-scroll), axis="y"\|"x", trigger="self"\|"parent", as, className, children` | reduced/mobile → static, no transform | linear scrub ±speed·300px across the trigger's viewport pass (counter-parallax vector) |
| `ChapterMarker` | `src/components/motion/ChapterMarker.jsx` | `chapters:[{id,label}], className` | reduced/mobile → plain list | mono chapter list; per-id ScrollTriggers (top/bottom center) toggle classes/`data-active` only — zero per-frame work |
| `Section` / `SectionHeader` | `src/components/motion/Section.jsx` | Section: `id, as, padded=true, bordered, className`; SectionHeader: `eyebrow, title, lead, className` | none needed (static layout, zero GSAP) | THE rhythm primitive: always `relative` (scar #4 host), `--k-section-pad` block padding, 1280px container. SectionHeader = mono eyebrow + display h2 + ink-dim lead |

<!-- appended by sprint 04 -->
**S04 rules + support files (binding for S05–S13):**
- **Page sprints compose ONLY these primitives — no ad-hoc GSAP in sections**
  except page-level glue registered via `useRouteScrollContext().add()`.
- **Lifecycle:** every primitive runs its GSAP inside its OWN `gsap.context`
  scoped to its root ref, reverted on unmount (StrictMode-idempotent, scar #7;
  safe when a section unmounts independently of the route, and everything still
  dies before the next route mounts — scar #9; proven by the verify.mjs
  lab→home trigger-death check). The route context + sentinel from S03 is
  unchanged; `__stCount()` baseline is still 1 on stub routes.
- **Static-tier gate:** `useMotionStatic()` / `useCoarsePointer()` from
  `src/components/motion/useMotionStatic.js` — every primitive renders its
  designed final state and marks the root `data-static="true"` on the static
  tier (QA keys off this; keep it when restyling).
- **Velocity:** `src/lib/scrollBus.js` — `scrollBus.velocity` (px/s, smoothed)
  + refcounted `subscribeScrollBus()`. ONE writer, driven by `gsap.ticker`.
  Never measure scroll velocity anywhere else (motion law 4).
- **Easing:** `src/lib/motion.js` adds `TRAVEL = 40` (law-14 minimum move).
  Still the ONE easing source; primitives contain zero raw beziers.
- **Barrel:** `src/components/motion/index.js` re-exports every primitive +
  the gate hooks (side-effect-free, tree-shakeable).
- **QA completion attrs:** `data-revealed`, `data-split`, `data-struck`,
  `data-latched`, `data-state`, `data-static` — verify.mjs asserts them on
  `/lab`; keep them wired when composing pages.

### Dev `/lab` proof bench (S04)
- `src/pages/Lab.jsx`, route `/lab` registered in `src/App.jsx` ONLY when
  `import.meta.env.DEV` (lazy import; Rollup DCEs it — prod bundle byte-count
  unchanged, no lab chunk, verified by build grep each sprint).
- Mounts every primitive once with QA hooks (`[data-lab="<name>"]` wrappers,
  `[data-lab-progress]` scrub readout). verify.mjs runs a 3-tier lab suite
  (desktop strikes/latch/fling, reduced static, mobile no-overflow);
  walkthrough.mjs captures `lab-desktop/mobile/reduced` frames.
  `node scripts/qa/verify.mjs --route=/lab` runs the lab suite alone.

**S03 implementation notes (the rows above are now LIVE):**
- `window.__lenis` — set whenever Lenis exists, i.e. NOT under reduced-motion
  or mobile (≤768): those tiers run native scroll and `__lenis` stays
  `undefined` **by design**; QA scripts must keep their `window.__lenis ? … :
  window.scrollTo` fallback.
- `window.__stCount()` — set at module scope of `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js`.
  Baseline is **1 per route** (the route-sentinel trigger, see backbone API
  below). verify.mjs asserts the 3×-cycle nav-leak invariant against it.
- `window.__preloaderDone` — `true` once the `preloaderDone` event has fired
  (all paths: played, session-skipped, `?skippreload`, reduced-motion).
- `?skippreload` / `?route=<path>` / `?jump=<px>` — honored app-wide
  (`?route`/`?jump` parsed once on load in `SmoothScroll`; `?jump` waits for
  `preloaderDone` + 0.5s before jumping).

## Scroll/motion backbone API (S03)
<!-- appended by sprint 03 -->

The backbone every later sprint composes on. Files:
`src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx` · `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js` ·
`src/lib/motion.js` · `src/components/Preloader.jsx`.

### `useRouteScrollContext()` — `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js`
```js
const { scopeRef, add, refreshSoon } = useRouteScrollContext();
```
- **EVERY page component (S06–S13) MUST call this** and register ALL its
  scroll work through it — no ad-hoc ScrollTriggers outside a route context.
- `scopeRef` → attach to the page's root element (plain block, never
  transformed — scar #2). All 5 current pages already do this.
- `add(fn)` → run `fn` inside the route's `gsap.context`. Safe to call from
  section effects that run BEFORE the page's context exists (child effects run
  first): callbacks queue and flush into the context at creation. Sections that
  unmount independently of the route must manage their own nested
  `gsap.context` + revert.
- On route unmount the context `revert()`s — every trigger/tween dies BEFORE
  the next route's effects run (scar #9, proven by the verify.mjs 3×-cycle
  assertion).
- The hook auto-creates one zero-cost **route-sentinel** ScrollTrigger
  (id `route-sentinel`) per route — that is why `__stCount()` baseline is 1,
  not 0. Do not remove it; the leak assertion measures against it.
- **Scar #1 reminder** (in the hook's JSDoc): never `self.kill()` inside
  `onUpdate`/`onToggle` — `gsap.delayedCall(0, () => self.kill())`.

### `refreshSoon(delaySec = 0.15)` — same module, also exported standalone
- THE one coordinated, debounced `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` channel (scar #3).
  Calls coalesce. Already wired: `document.fonts.ready` + `preloaderDone` →
  refresh; every route mount → refresh; nav scroll-reset → refresh.
- **S13 calls `refreshSoon()` after its transition-in completes.** Sections
  NEVER call `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` directly.

### SmoothScroll provider — `src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx`
- Mounted ONCE in `src/main.jsx` (inside `BrowserRouter`, outside the Outlet).
  Lenis scrolls the REAL window, driven from `gsap.ticker`
  (`lagSmoothing(0)`); survives navigation; on route change does
  `lenis.scrollTo(0, { immediate: true })` + `refreshSoon()`.
- `useLenis()` context hook → the live instance (null on native tiers/early).
- Reduced-motion OR mobile (≤768) ⇒ **no Lenis at all** (native scroll tier).

### Preloader — `src/components/Preloader.jsx` (mounted in `RootLayout`)
- `[data-preloader]` veil, z-[100]; "All Is. Now" assemble + 0→100 counter on
  the drafting grid; pure CSS/GSAP, no deps. Total ≈2.4s.
- Once per session: sessionStorage key **`k-preloaded`** (set on completion
  AND on every skip path).
- Skip paths (render null, no veil): session flag · `?skippreload` ·
  `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- **`preloaderDone`** — `window` CustomEvent dispatched on EVERY path
  (completed or skipped) + `window.__preloaderDone = true`. The refresh
  coordinator and `?jump` wait on it. S13's transition system must NOT gate on
  the preloader existing — gate on the event/flag.
- Scroll-lock while shown: `documentElement.style.overflow = "hidden"` +
  `lenis.stop()`; both restored on exit.

### Motion constants — `src/lib/motion.js` (TOKENS §5, live)
- `EASE.PRIMARY` = CustomEase **"kPrimary"** (`cubic-bezier(0.7,0,0.2,1)`,
  registered at module load) · `EASE.SETTLE` = `"expo.out"` · `EASE.SNAP` =
  `"back.out(1.4)"` (never on opacity). CSS twin: `EASE_CSS_PRIMARY`.
- `DUR.XS/SM/MD/LG/SCENE` = 0.3/0.5/0.6/0.7/1.0s · `STAGGER.LINE/CELL` =
  0.06/0.04. One easing language — import from here only (motion law 1).
- Legacy exports (`EASE_OUT`, `revealVariants`, …) remain for pre-rebuild
  components only; they die with the old components (S05+).

## Global chrome contract (S05)
<!-- appended by sprint 05 -->

The persistent app shell. `src/pages/RootLayout.jsx` renders, in order:
`<Preloader/>` + `<CustomCursor/>` (gated) + `<Navbar/>` + `<main id="main">`
(relative — the S13 wipe-overlay host, scar #4) wrapping the transition seam +
`<Footer/>`. Routed page content mounts in the seam BETWEEN Navbar and Footer.
S02's `StubNav.jsx` / `StubFooter.jsx` are DELETED — superseded by the chrome
below.

| piece | path | contract |
|---|---|---|
| Navbar | `src/components/chrome/Navbar.jsx` | `<NavLink>` rows from `NAV` + MagneticButton Calendly CTA from `LINKS.calendly`. Condense-on-scroll = ONE shell-level ScrollTrigger (`id: "navbar-condense"`, start 64 → "max") flipping `data-condensed` on the `<header>` — lives in the component's OWN `gsap.context`, **NEVER in `useRouteScrollContext`** (a per-route context would revert it on first nav and freeze the navbar). Mobile toggle = `[data-nav-toggle]`, menu panel = `#mobile-menu` (conditional render, instant). |
| Footer | `src/components/chrome/Footer.jsx` | Global on every route. Hosts `#newsletter` (inside `<footer>`), the Sender mount `[data-sender-form-id="axkAXn"]` (+ `sender-form-field` class) with the LOCKED polling-render lifecycle (`window.senderForms.render/destroy`, 100ms × 50 poll, StrictMode-safe: cancel + destroy + `replaceChildren()` on cleanup), the ONE company-LinkedIn link, `mailto:hello@kartar.ai`, a Calendly CTA, NAV links — all from `src/data/*`. |
| CustomCursor | `src/components/chrome/CustomCursor.jsx` | Thin alias over `src/components/motion/MagneticCursor.jsx` — ONE cursor implementation, mounted ONCE in RootLayout (singleton overlay; the S04 `/lab` local mount was removed). Gated: renders `null` on coarse pointer / mobile (<768) / reduced-motion. Ring node carries `data-magnetic-cursor`. |
| Wordmark | `src/components/chrome/Wordmark.jsx` | `BRAND.wordmark` → "kartar" + accent "AI". No saffron (anti-wander rule 1). |
| CTA→newsletter helper | `src/lib/goToNewsletter.js` → `goToNewsletter()` | THE shared helper (locked behavior). Footer is global so it works from ANY route: `window.__lenis.scrollTo("#newsletter", {offset:-24})`, `scrollIntoView` fallback (instant under reduced-motion). Returns `false` if the anchor is missing. Dev/QA global: `window.__goToNewsletter` (DEV-only, DCE'd from prod). **S06's CtaBanner imports this — never hand-roll the scroll.** Home's stub `#cta` button is already wired through it. |

asserts `transform: none` + `overflowY: visible`, scar #2), `data-condensed`
(navbar scroll state), `[data-nav-toggle]`, `#mobile-menu`,
`[data-cursor="magnet"]`.

**Perf note:** `framer-motion` (existing dep) now ships in the initial bundle
for the seam — initial JS 167.07 KB gz (budget ≤ 220 KB). Still zero `three`
bytes on every route.

## Transition timing
<!-- appended by sprint 13 -->

S13 fills the page-transition timing so QA waits long enough and confirms no
trigger leak across nav (scar #9). Reduced-motion = instant cut.

- Transition system home: _(S13)_
- Total in+out duration (ms) QA must wait: _(S13)_
- Refresh/kill ordering across the seam: _(S13)_

<!-- appended by sprint 05 (the structural seam S13 fills) -->
**S05 seam (live, no-op):** `src/pages/RootLayout.jsx` — `<AnimatePresence
mode="wait" initial={false}>` keyed on `location.pathname` around a
`motion.div[data-route-seam]` containing a **FrozenOutlet** (captures
`useOutlet()` once per mount so an exiting child keeps rendering the OLD page —
prevents double-mount through the swap and preserves scar-#9 ordering: old
route's context reverts before the new route's effects).
- The seam wrapper must NEVER gain transform/overflow/height that captures
  scroll (scar #2) — verify.mjs asserts this per route.
- The seam does NOT kill triggers — that stays in `useRouteScrollContext`'s
  revert. S13 adds the wipe as an `absolute inset-0` overlay on the relative
  `<main id="main">` and calls `lenis.scrollTo(0,{immediate:true})` +
  `refreshSoon()` at cover-peak — the commented **"S13 TRANSITION HOOK POINT"**
  marker in RootLayout.jsx is the exact spot.
- Gate transitions on `window.__preloaderDone` / the `preloaderDone` event,
  never on the preloader node existing.

content.content:
1	# DIRECTION — "Blueprint → Built" (the binding art direction)
2	
3	**Winner: Route A — Blueprint → Built** (JUDGE.md, unanimous 3/3, composite
4	4.72 vs C 4.25 / B 2.73). The site is a **drafting table where blueprints
5	resolve into built reality**: ghost wireframe linework, dimension marks and
6	annotations exist first; as you move, they **ink in**, fill with material, and
7	snap into finished objects. The feeling sold is *competence that makes things
8	real* — technical drawing meets product photography, the quiet luxury of a
9	well-made tool. The proof-of-look is `plans/design/styleframes/route-A.html`
10	(+ `route-A.png` / `route-A-full.png`); graft from runner-up C only where noted
11	in §Motion Language.
12	
13	Authority: `PROTOCOL > CONTRACTS > this doc + TOKENS.md > sprint files`.
14	
15	---
16	
17	## Tokens
18	
19	Full machine source: **`plans/design/TOKENS.md`** (S02 implements it verbatim;
20	CSS var prefix `--k-`). Summary:
21	
22	- **Palette** — ground `#0C0D10` graphite · surfaces `#15171C`/`#101216` ·
23	  lines `#23262D`/`#2E323B` · ink `#EDE7DA` warm bone (+ dim `#C9C3B6`) ·
24	  muted `#7C7F87`/`#4A4D55` · **one accent `#3B7CFF` blueprint blue**
25	  (+ strong `#2E63D6`, soft `rgba(59,124,255,0.14)`). Accent ≤ 10% of any
26	  frame; greys + bone carry all mass; body copy never in accent or `muted-2`.
27	- **Type** — Display **Clash Display** 600/700; Body **Satoshi** 400/500/700;
28	  Mono **JetBrains Mono** 400/500 (dimension marks, stamps, traces, all
29	  numbers `tnum`). Self-hosted WOFF2, 4 files ≤ 220 KB, `font-display: swap`,
30	  no CDN fonts in the shipped app.
31	- **Type scale** — hero `clamp(3.25rem, 8.5vw, 7.5rem)`; display
32	  `clamp(2.25rem, 4.5vw, 4rem)`; body-lg `clamp(1.0625rem, 1.4vw, 1.25rem)`;
33	  body `1rem/1.7`; mono-label `0.6875rem`, tracking `0.12em`, uppercase.
34	- **Motion constants** — `EASE.PRIMARY = cubic-bezier(0.7, 0, 0.2, 1)`
35	  (CustomEase `"kPrimary"`), `EASE.SETTLE = "expo.out"`,
36	  `EASE.SNAP = "back.out(1.4)"` (grid-lock only); durations 0.3/0.5/0.6/0.7s,
37	  scene-settle 1.0s; route wipe ≤ 700ms; reduced-motion cross-fade ≤ 200ms.
38	- **Layout** — container 1280px; section pad `clamp(6rem, 12vh, 10rem)`;
39	  visible drafting grid pitch 48px; radii 2/6/10px (drafted, near-sharp).
40	
41	## IA
42	
43	**LOCKED 4-route map** (brief §2; S02 implements with `react-router-dom`,
44	`BrowserRouter` + GitHub Pages SPA fallback: `public/404.html` sessionStorage
45	redirect trick + restore snippet in `index.html`; keep `CNAME` at root AND
46	`public/CNAME`):
47	
48	| Route | Path | Purpose | Content (all facts survive — see PROTOCOL §8 / facts.json) |
49	|---|---|---|---|
50	| Home | `/` | The cinematic pitch | Hero ("All Is. Now", dual CTA Calendly + See our work) · **H2 CustomsIQ pipeline showpiece** · 8-industries marquee · 3 services · 3 testimonials · CTA banner · footer |
51	| Work | `/work` | Proof | 2 case studies (horizontal chapters) · **W2 9-industry skyline showpiece** · recap CTA |
52	| Studio | `/studio` | Who builds it | 4-step process (axis rail) · 18-member team grid · manifesto |
53	| Contact | `/contact` | Convert | EmailJS form (locked ids) · Calendly · `hello@kartar.ai` |
54	
55	**Per-route act order** (locked; travel vectors per §Motion Language):
56	
57	- **Home:** H0 preloader ("All Is. Now" strokes draw + 0→100 counter, once per
58	  session) → H1 hero (headline lands by line; SVG polyhedron idles; bg drafting
59	  grid counter-parallaxes diagonally) → H2 ★ CustomsIQ pipeline (pinned
60	  assemble/disassemble) → H3 industries marquee (8, horizontal,
61	  velocity-coupled) → H4 services (3 cards arrive from right, stack + lock) →
62	  H5 testimonials (3, horizontal filmstrip) → H6 CTA banner (type scales in
63	  depth; magnetic Calendly button).
64	- **Work:** W1 case studies (2, horizontal pinned chapters traveling left;
65	  metrics count-strike on center) → W2 ★ industry-solutions skyline (9 volumes
66	  rise under pin while field pans) → W3 recap + magnetic CTA → Contact.
67	- **Studio:** S1 process axis-rail (marker travels a diagonal/horizontal ruler;
68	  4 steps pin and engrave) → S2 team grid (18; diagonal stagger;
69	  alternating-column parallax; initials-fallback avatars; LinkedIn hover) → S3
70	  manifesto kinetic type.
71	- **Contact:** C1 split — form panel slides from left, CTA panel from right.
72	  EmailJS ids/fields/success text byte-exact per CONTRACTS.
73	
74	**Globals:** Navbar (Home/Work/Studio/Contact from `src/data/nav.js`) + footer
75	(Sender form `axkAXn` at `#newsletter`, LinkedIn company, Calendly from
76	`src/data/links.js`). All content imports from `src/data/*` — the facts
77	contract made executable; no hand-typed content strings in components.
78	**Page transition (locked mechanism):** horizontal **chapter-wipe** — a
79	full-bleed *blueprint-paper sheet* (bone `#EDE7DA` with faint grid + title-block
80	stamp) sweeps across, covers, swaps content at cover-peak, reveals out; total
81	≤ 700ms; scroll resets to top under cover; reduced-motion = instant cross-fade
82	≤ 200ms. Implemented as overlay only — never a scroll container (scar #2).
83	
84	## Motion Language
85	
86	**Thesis: the page is not scrolling — things are being drafted, inked and
87	built.** Ghost wireframes ink in and snap to a solid built state on
88	`EASE.PRIMARY`, 0.5–0.7s, mechanical precision, slight overshoot then LOCK.
89	Never bouncy, never floaty. "Forged-stays-forged": strikes hold their end state
90	forever; only H2/W1/W2 are explicitly reversible.
91	
92	**Multi-directional doctrine (locked):** every act declares a dominant travel
93	vector that is NOT plain vertical; ≥ 3 acts per route move horizontally or in
94	depth. Route A's vectors: horizontal (pipeline rail L→R, marquee, filmstrips,
95	case-study chapters), depth (polyhedron wireframe→solid extrude toward viewer,
96	CTA type scaling), centripetal (graph nodes pulling inward to HS 8471.30),
97	counter-parallax (the drafting grid drifts opposite scroll, bg vs fg at
98	different rates). Graft from C: the industries band may use the single allowed
99	skew-on-velocity; index/table-of-contents rhythm (numbered rows, hairline
100	rules) is part of the vocabulary.
101	
102	**Primitive table** (S04 builds these in `src/components/motion/*`; page
103	sprints compose ONLY these):
104	
105	| Primitive | What it does | Reduced/mobile fallback | Scar gate |
106	|---|---|---|---|
107	| `InkReveal` | element strikes ghost→inked (opacity/clip + 40px travel), holds forever | static final state | #7, #8 |
108	| `DraftLine` | SVG stroke-dashoffset line/edge draw | fully-drawn static | #8 |
109	| `SplitLines` | headline lands line-by-line (waits `document.fonts.ready`) | static text | #3, #7 |
110	| `SnapStack` | cards arrive from right, stack with slight rotation, snap-lock | plain stacked cards, fade-up | #4, #5, #8 |
111	| `AxisRail` | pinned scrub: marker travels a rail, panels advance laterally | native vertical stack, no pin | #1, #2, #5, #9 |
112	| `KineticMarquee` | xPercent marquee coupled to shared `scrollBus.velocity` | slow CSS marquee / static list | #8, #10 |
113	| `MagneticButton` | cursor-magnet CTA (heavier retune) | disabled on touch; normal tap target | #7 |
114	| `CountStrike` | numbers count up when centered, then hold | final number rendered immediately | #5, #8 |
115	| `DimensionMark` | mono annotation + leader line fades/draws in | static annotation | #8 |
116	
117	**The 14 motion laws** (brief §5.1 — LOCKED, condensed): (1) one easing
118	language from `src/lib/motion.js` only; (2) scrub over trigger; trigger-once
119	strikes hold forever; (3) high-water latch — reveal progress only grows;
120	(4) one shared `scrollBus.velocity` writer; (5) no standalone rAF — everything
121	off `gsap.ticker`/`useFrame`; (6) zero per-frame allocations; (7) no per-frame
122	`getBoundingClientRect` — cache on refresh/resize; (8) Lenis scrolls the REAL
123	window; (9) never `self.kill()` inside `onUpdate` — defer via
124	`gsap.delayedCall`; (10) ONE coordinated `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` after fonts
125	+ preloader + lazy settle; (11) `position: relative` on any section hosting an
126	`absolute inset-0` veil; (12) prefer utilities — injected `<style>` outranks
127	Tailwind, scope it; (13) StrictMode-idempotent effects, `gsap.context()` +
128	revert; (14) nothing under 0.3s; minimum perceptible move 40px.
129	
130	**The 10 scars are protocol** (PROTOCOL §2 — restated so every sprint
131	inherits): #1 defer ScrollTrigger kills out of `onUpdate`; #2 real-window
132	scroll, transitions are overlays not scroll containers; #3 one debounced
133	refresh after fonts/preloader/lazy settle; #4 `relative` under `inset-0`
134	overlays; #5 `onLeave`/`onLeaveBack` latches so flings commit end states;
135	#6 scope injected CSS; #7 idempotent effects + GL disposal under StrictMode;
136	#8 `gsap.matchMedia`, mobile ≤ 768 and `prefers-reduced-motion` gates never
137	regress, designed static states never blanks; #9 per-route `gsap.context`,
138	kill old route triggers BEFORE new mount, refresh after transition-in,
139	`lenis.scrollTo(0, { immediate: true })`; #10 section-local canvases
140	unmount+dispose on route-leave, `frameloop="demand"`, zero per-frame
141	allocations.
142	
143	**Tiers (locked degrade matrix):** `full / mid / lite / none` resolved via
144	`src/lib/useReducedMotion.js` (breakpoint 768). Mobile/lite: no pins, no
145	horizontal scroll, no 3D canvases, zero `three` bytes, zero horizontal overflow
146	at 390px. Reduced/none: designed static final states. The static design IS the
147	baseline; motion is additive.
148	
149	## Showpieces
150	
151	Exactly **two WebGL showpieces ship** (H2 + W2). H1 is **decided: downgraded to
152	CSS/SVG** (brief allows; removes the third GL surface and keeps Home's initial
153	bundle lean). S10/S11/S12 read these briefs:
154	
155	1. **H2 ★ — CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble** (Home, mount
156	   `[data-showpiece="pipeline"]`). Pinned GSAP-scrubbed scene (replaces the old
157	   hand-rolled 480vh black box). As you scroll, drafted fragments — invoice/doc
158	   nodes — **fly in from the four screen edges and assemble centripetally** into
159	   the knowledge graph stage-by-stage: Read → Ground (Ch.84/85/rulings) →
160	   Reason (GIR, AIDC) → Classify (**HS 8471.30** crystallizes at center) → Cite
161	   (Notif. node + citation lines draw). Scroll back = disassembles in reverse.
162	   Material language: nodes are drafted circles (instanced meshes), edges are
